surface_verification.justification: Change is a Python compliance-scoring library (_grade_gate / _grade_types / control_grade / _traceability / _control_block) plus a CI workflow and docs; no startable user-facing web/UI surface in this repo. Verified by the compliance suite (919), shared + cross-plugin integration (174), and a live-data probe (collect_all -> build_grade_inputs -> compute_grade) yielding the intended honest B/89 with 'traceability declining'. The WebUI that renders the dashboard is a separate repo (propagation is a named fast-follow).

behaviors.behavior: Self-relative decline severity (freeze / relative-drop / stable-low / improvement)
Capped (<=35%) traceability-decline penalty, never zeroing the dimension
A traceability decline caps the headline below A with a 'declining' reason
A non-binding decline does NOT claim 'Capped:' (raw already below ceiling)
A dark EXPECTED control caps below A and flags 'verification incomplete'; unexpected stays A
A collapsed (F-band) load-bearing pillar caps the headline to F
The decline penalty NEVER triggers the F-collapse cap (review #1 regression)
A broken supporting/excluded dim does not hard-cap; one open critical is a gap not a cap
Anchors cite no safety-critical standard and exactly one open standard each
The adapter lights the fr_tag trend + the security expectation from repo state
fr_tag_trend SSoT keeps the dashboard 'Recent changes traced to an FR' row byte-identical
Back-compat: with no trend/expectation signal the pure scorer behaves identically
control_grade.py value model split to _grade_types.py imports/re-exports cleanly
behaviors.evidence: full compliance suite 919 PASSED + ruff clean

behaviors.behavior: scorecard.yml runs the native ossf/scorecard-action in CI
behaviors.note: GitHub Actions runtime; YAML parse verified, SHA-pinned @v2.4.3, repo confirmed PUBLIC, job guarded to canonical repo.

degraded.reason: check-external-review-keys.py not present at the expected shared/scripts/tools path in this worktree; external review treated as advisory/opt-out. Mitigated by an independent adversarial opus code review (1 blocker found + fixed) + full suite + ruff.
